Real user-facing a11y/i18n defect for screen-reader users; consistent with many accepted aria-label entries in rounds 4/7. Not a duplicate: the only ledger entry for this file is round 7 at line 754 (visible '+ Add block' text and 'Remove block N' aria-label) — a different line and a different element. Not vendored: this is packages-js/fields-advanced, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*. Confidence high.

confirmed.title: AdminRefundController 'errors' detail strings hardcoded English (already-refunded)
confirmed.detail: The top-level 'message' is localized via __('arqel::messages.marketplace.validation_failed'), but the nested validation-error detail string 'errors' => ['purchase' => ['Purchase is already refunded.']] is hardcoded English. This array is serialized verbatim in the 422 JsonResponse and rendered as the per-field validation error in the React UI. Framework-hardcoded. Ledger covered the 'message' key (line 41/62) but NOT these nested 'errors' detail strings.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed in code at /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages/marketplace/src/Http/Controllers/AdminRefundController.php lines 47 and 54: nested validation-error detail strings are hardcoded English ('Purchase is already refunded.' and 'Only completed purchases can be refunded.') while the sibling top-level 'message' key on the same 422 JsonResponse IS localized via __('arqel::messages.marketplace.validation_failed'). This proves the file is i18n-aware and the nested strings were simply missed. The response is serialized verbatim with HTTP 422 and rendered as per-field validation errors in the React admin UI, so it is genuinely user-facing. Ledger scan: Round 9 contains three AdminRefundController-adjacent entries — line 41 ('Purchase not found' message key), line 62 ('Refund failed at gateway'), and a PluginPurchaseController 'Validation failed' top-level message-key entry. None of them cover the nested 'errors' => ['purchase' => [...]] detail arrays. Grep for 'already', 'completed', 'errors', 'nested', 'detail' across the ledger returns no matching entry. The file lives in packages/marketplace/src, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*, so it is not a vendored shadcn primitive. Real, novel, user-facing, non-vendored.

This 'errors' array follows Laravel's validation-error JSON shape (field => [messages]) that Inertia/React surfaces verbatim in form validation displays, so it is genuinely user-facing. It is framework code (packages/marketplace), not a vendored shadcn primitive. Ledger check: the only AdminRefundController entries are line 41 ('Purchase not found') and line 62 ('Refund failed at gateway'), both round 9; no ledger entry covers line 47 or 54 of this file (the other line-47/54 entries belong to DataTable, TableFilters, TenantSwitcher, ArqelCreatePage, FieldRenderer, FieldSearchController, etc.). Therefore not a duplicate. (Note: line 47 'Purchase is already refunded.' is an adjacent instance of the same uncovered pattern, also unledgered, but the finding correctly targets line 54.)

confirmed.title: TenantNotFoundException default message hardcoded English, serialized to 404 render
confirmed.detail: The constructor default $message = 'No tenant could be resolved for the request.' is hardcoded English and is NOT routed through __()/trans(). ResolveTenantMiddleware (line 44) throws it with only the identifier, so this default is what reaches users. render() serializes $this->getMessage() into the JSON 'message' (line 38), the Inertia 'arqel::errors.tenant-not-found' payload (line 47), and the plain 404 body (line 55). The denial/abort messages in RequireTenantFeature were localized in prior rounds, but this resolver-failure 404 message was missed. Framework-hardcoded.
confirmed.why_real:
Verified end-to-end. TenantNotFoundException.php:29 hardcodes the English constructor default $message = 'No tenant could be resolved for the request.' and never routes it through __()/trans(). ResolveTenantMiddleware.php:44 throws the exception with only the named `identifier:` argument (no $message), so the hardcoded default is what reaches users. render() serializes getMessage() verbatim into the JSON 404 body (line 38/43), the Inertia 'arqel::errors.tenant-not-found' payload (line 38/47), and the plain Symfony 404 body (line 55) — all three user-facing surfaces emit untranslated English. This is a genuine user-facing i18n defect on a framework-owned PHP file.

Not a duplicate: the ledger contains RequireTenantFeature.php (lines 663, 1071: 'no_current_tenant' and 'feature_unavailable') and TenantSwitcher.tsx (lines 285, 321), but NOT TenantNotFoundException.php. The finding correctly notes the denial/feature messages were localized in prior rounds while this resolver-failure 404 was missed. The sibling RequireTenantFeature uses __('arqel::messages.tenant.*'), confirming the established framework i18n convention this file violates.

Not vendored: file is under packages/tenant/src/Exceptions/, a first-party Arqel framework package, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*.

confirmed.title: AiTextField default buttonLabel 'Generate with AI' hardcoded English
confirmed.detail: protected string $buttonLabel = 'Generate with AI' is the PHP-side default, serialized via toArray() as 'buttonLabel' => $this->buttonLabel (line 188) and rendered on the React AI text-field button. Not routed through __()/trans(). The JS AI input components had aria/error strings localized in prior rounds, but this PHP-supplied default button face is the visible label and remains English unless an app calls ->buttonLabel(). Framework-hardcoded default.
confirmed.why_real:
Verified at /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages/ai/src/Fields/AiTextField.php:40 — `protected string $buttonLabel = 'Generate with AI';` is a hardcoded English PHP default. It is serialized verbatim at line 188 (`'buttonLabel' => $this->buttonLabel`) inside getTypeSpecificProps(); grep confirms NO __()/trans()/localize call anywhere in the file. The React consumer at packages-js/ai/src/AiTextInput.tsx:111-115 reads `serverLabel = fieldProps?.buttonLabel` and only falls back to the localized `t('arqel.ai.generate', DEFAULT_BUTTON_LABEL)` when serverLabel is undefined/empty. Because the PHP default is always the non-empty string 'Generate with AI', the serverLabel branch always wins and the round-4 JS localization fallback is dead in the default case — so the rendered button face stays English regardless of locale unless an app explicitly calls ->buttonLabel(). User-facing (visible primary button on AI text fields).

Not a duplicate: the only related ledger entry is round 4 at packages-js/ai/src/AiTextInput.tsx:57 (JS-side DEFAULT_BUTTON_LABEL constant). No ledger entry references AiTextField.php, the PHP buttonLabel property, or getTypeSpecificProps serialization. This is the distinct PHP-side layer that pre-empts the JS fix — the same 'PHP default overrides localized JS fallback' family the ledger tracks for other fields but never for this one.

Not a vendored shadcn primitive — it lives in packages/ai/src/Fields/, framework source.

confirmed.title: AiExtractField default buttonLabel 'Extract with AI' hardcoded English
confirmed.detail: protected string $buttonLabel = 'Extract with AI' default, serialized via toArray() ('buttonLabel' => $this->buttonLabel, line 225) to the React AI extract-field button face. Not routed through __()/trans(). Framework-hardcoded default; not in ledger.
confirmed.why_real:
CONFIRMED. AiExtractField.php:49 defaults `protected string $buttonLabel = 'Extract with AI'` (hardcoded English, no __()/trans()) and always serializes it via getTypeSpecificProps() at line 225 ('buttonLabel' => $this->buttonLabel). The React component AiExtractInput.tsx:149-153 prioritizes this server label over its own i18n fallback: `serverLabel !== undefined && serverLabel !== '' ? serverLabel : t('arqel.ai.extract', 'Extract with AI')`. Because the PHP default is always non-empty, the hardcoded English string permanently shadows the frontend translation key arqel.ai.extract — a non-English locale can never localize this button face (rendered as visible <span> text at line 275 AND aria-label at line 268) unless the developer manually overrides buttonLabel per-field. Genuinely user-facing and reproduces.

NOT vendored: file is packages/ai/src/Fields/AiExtractField.php, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*.

NOT a duplicate: grepped the full ledger (rounds 1-14). No entry references AiExtractField.php or the PHP-side buttonLabel default. The nearest entries are distinct: AiTextInput.tsx:57 (R4) is the FRONTEND default-label fix (the very t() fallback that this PHP default shadows — different file/layer); the AiClassify/Generate/AnalyzeImage controllers (R8/9) are controllers, not the field; and the verbatim-serialization family (R1) covered Field.php/Column.php/Action.php/TernaryFilter.php but never AiExtractField::$buttonLabel. This is a novel, uncovered instance of that systemic family.

confirmed.title: AiImageField default buttonLabel 'Analyze with AI' hardcoded English
confirmed.detail: protected string $buttonLabel = 'Analyze with AI' default, serialized via toArray() ('buttonLabel' => $this->buttonLabel, line 226) to the React AI image-field button face. Not routed through __()/trans(). Framework-hardcoded default; not in ledger.
confirmed.why_real:
CONFIRMED. The finding reproduces exactly. packages/ai/src/Fields/AiImageField.php:69 declares `protected string $buttonLabel = 'Analyze with AI';` and getTypeSpecificProps() at line 226 serializes it verbatim (`'buttonLabel' => $this->buttonLabel`) with no __()/trans() wrapping. It reaches the React AI image-field button face and ALSO actively shadows i18n: AiImageInput.tsx:174-178 prefers the server-provided label over the translation — `serverLabel !== undefined && serverLabel !== '' ? serverLabel : t('arqel.ai.analyze', 'Analyze with AI')`. Because PHP always serializes its non-empty 'Analyze with AI' default, serverLabel is never empty in the default case, so the React `t()` path is dead and a Spanish/PT-BR panel renders English. Genuine, reproducible, user-facing.

NOT a duplicate. The nearest ledger entry is round 4 (packages-js/ai/src/AiTextInput.tsx:57, 'AI field default button labels hardcoded English ... Analyze with AI ...') — but that is the REACT-side fallback string in a different file/package/layer (packages-js/ai). The PHP source AiImageField::$buttonLabel that overrides that fallback is in packages/ai (PHP) and is NOT in the ledger anywhere (grep for AiImageField / buttonLabel / 'packages/ai/src/Fields' returns no ledger hit). It matches the firmly-established ledger pattern of distinct 'PHP $label serialized verbatim, never through __()' findings (Field::$label R1, Column::$label R1, Action::$label R1, Widget heading R2, many *Field PHP labels R2/R7), which are all treated as separate confirmed bugs.

NOT a vendored shadcn primitive — it is framework field code in packages/ai, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*.
